why: Goaltending is binary (good night vs bad night)
±3 goals = 30-50% swing in over/under outcome
Hard to predict pre-game (late scratches, backup decisions)
Example: Expected 210.5 total, goalie has .880 save % → could be 207 or 215
additional_considerations: Should also consider: Backup goalie decision timing
Consider: Injury-affected team depth (missing 1-2 key forwards changes pace)

why: 350+ teams = huge quality variance (Duke vs mid-major)
Freshman rotations = unpredictable contributions (transfers still learning)
Blowout risk: 30-40% of games have 15+ point margins
Mid-major teams can have 70+ point nights (vs power conference)
Transfer portal adds uncertainty (players new to system)
additional_considerations: Could enable SLAM for: Top-50 RPI teams only (more predictable)
Could enable SLAM for: Tournament games (seeding reduces blowouts)
Season timing: Enable by March (rotations stabilized)
CBB.recommendation: KEEP SLAM DISABLED for regular season, enable post-January

why: 156 players = 156 independent outcomes
Weather variance: ±5 mph wind = ±1-2 strokes impact
Altitude changes (Denver vs coastal) = ±1.5 strokes
Course setup (firm greens vs soft) = ±2 strokes variance
Cuts happen at 75% of field (sudden elimination risk)
additional_considerations: Could enable SLAM for: Majors only (more data, lower variance)
Could enable SLAM for: PGA Tour events (not web.com)
Weather: Forecast must be stable 3+ days out
Golf.recommendation: KEEP SLAM DISABLED for web.com, enable for majors/PGA Tour

why: 82-game season = 400+ picks per season calibration data
Rotations locked (coaches rarely change mid-season)
Injuries reported clearly (DNP-CD, Out, Questionable, Probable)
Same matchups repeat 4x (season + playoffs = predicability)
Star player load management is KNOWN (not surprised)
NBA.confidence_level: HIGH - NBA is your gold standard
NBA.recommendation: KEEP SLAM ENABLED - NBA is most predictable

why: ✅ 17-game season gives reasonable data volume
✅ Rotations are more stable than college
❌ But: Only 17 games = less data than NBA (need 3-5 years history)
❌ Weather uncertainty (especially late season)
❌ Playoff scenarios change incentives
concerns: Single player injury can swing game 14+ points
Backup QB situations harder to predict than NBA bench rotations
NFL.recommendation: CONSIDER: Enable SLAM, but with tighter CI (wider confidence intervals)

why: Elo provides historical head-to-head data
Surface adjustment (clay/grass/hard) is well-studied
1v1 sport = fewer confounding variables
Ranking changes are transparent (ATP/WTA public)
additional_considerations: BUT: Weather at tournament site affects play style
BUT: Injury history (returning from injury affects play)
BUT: Tournament tier matters (Masters vs Challenger)
Tennis.recommendation: KEEP SLAM ENABLED - tennis is reliable

probability_model_strategy.overall_assessment: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ EXCELLENT - Correct distributions chosen per sport

why: Gaussian = continuous, unbounded distributions
NBA points are quasi-continuous (10-150 range)
NFL yards continuous (0-500+ range)
Golf strokes continuous (60-85 range)
Central limit theorem applies (many small effects)
implementation_notes: Should use: Truncated Normal (can't have negative points)
Should use: Heavy-tailed variant if outliers frequent
Current approach: Good, but consider t-distribution for tail risk
gaussian_model_sports.recommendation: KEEP Gaussian, consider Student-t for robustness

why: Poisson = count data (discrete, low-rate events)
Hockey: 2-7 goals per team (low count, high variance)
CBB: Team scoring is countable but varies wildly
Soccer: 0-3 goals typical (classic Poisson use case)
These sports: Scoring is rare enough that Poisson fits
implementation_notes: Poisson assumes: Events independent at constant rate (mostly true)
CBB caveat: Variance > mean (over-dispersion) → use Negative Binomial
Soccer: Goal sequences not fully independent (momentum), but Poisson OK
poisson_model_sports.recommendation: UPGRADE CBB: Switch from Poisson to Negative Binomial for over-dispersion

why: CBB: Variance in scoring is HUGE (60-120 point range)
Poisson assumes variance = mean, but CBB variance >> mean
Over-dispersion happens when: Weak team plays strong team (blowout)
Negative Binomial handles this (r + p parameters, not just λ)
expected_improvement: Tail risk: Better calibration on >10 point favorite bets
negative_binomial_recommendation.effort: 4-6 hours per sport (scipy.stats has Neg Binomial)
negative_binomial_recommendation.recommendation: PRIORITY: Upgrade CBB to Neg Binomial (Phase 5B or 5C)

hard_gates_assessment.assessment: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ EXCELLENT - Multiple validation layers prevent bad picks
what_gates_exist: ✅ Confidence interval checks (no NaN, NULL values)
what_gates_are_missing: ❌ Liquidity check (is this book actually taking this bet?)
❌ Line availability check (is the line still available?)
❌ Public money check (is this against the consensus?)
❌ Correlated bet detection (are multiple picks too similar?)
❌ Sharp money check (are the sharp bettors on other side?)

priority_1_high.why: Can't recommend bet that doesn't exist

priority_1_high.implementation: Verify line exists and spread hasn't moved >2%
priority_1_high.why: Pick is only good at THAT line

priority_2_medium.implementation: Check if multiple picks share same players/games
priority_2_medium.why: Two picks on same game = hidden correlation

priority_2_medium.implementation: Compare vs DFS public ownership tracking

priority_3_nice_to_have.implementation: Monitor which way early/big money moving
priority_3_nice_to_have.why: Sharp bettors have edge, follow their money

Phase 5B: Add tournament tier adjustment (Masters > Challenger)
Phase 5B: Add injury detection (returning players underperform)
Phase 6: Add surface momentum (clay specialists vs hard court)
Phase 5A: Enable SLAM for majors only (more data, lower variance)
Phase 5B: Add weather forecast integration (3-day stability check)
Phase 5B: Add field strength metric (tournament quality rating)
Phase 5B: Add goalie-specific priors (backup vs starter)

CBB.current_state: SLAM disabled (correct), but needs distribution fix
improvements: Phase 5B: PRIORITY - Switch to Negative Binomial (over-dispersion)
Phase 5B: Add RPI-based confidence tiers (top-50 teams more stable)
Phase 6: Add tournament mode (March Madness specific)

what_works: ✅ Probability models: Right distributions for each sport
✅ NBA as gold standard: Appropriate comparison
what_needs_work: ⚠️ CBB should use Negative Binomial not Poisson (Phase 5B)
⚠️ Calibration should be segmented not aggregate (Phase 5B)
⚠️ NFL SLAM threshold could be tighter (Phase 5C)
